question.text: Scenario: Vikram Mehta, a CFA charterholder with 7 years of experience in wealth management at a private bank, wants to set up his own investment advisory firm. He plans to register as an individual Investment Adviser with SEBI under the SEBI (Investment Advisers) Regulations, 2013. He has a net worth of Rs. 8 lakh. His wife Neha is an AMFI-registered mutual fund distributor who currently serves 50 clients. Vikram expects to start with 100 clients, charging an annual fee of Rs. 25,000 per client. One of his prospective clients, Mr. Rao, has assets worth Rs. 2 crore and wants both advisory and distribution services from the same family. Under SEBI IA Regulations, an individual Investment Adviser must have a minimum net worth of Rs. 5 lakh, can serve a maximum of 300 clients, and must convert to a non-individual IA if clients exceed 300 or advisory fees exceed Rs. 3 crore in a financial year. A part-time Investment Adviser is limited to 75 clients. Accepted qualifications include CFA Charter, post-graduate diploma in finance, or a professional qualification such as CA/CS/CWA with relevant experience. The IA must hold a valid NISM Investment Adviser certification. Records of investment advice, rationale, and client interactions must be maintained for a minimum of 5 years. An annual compliance audit by a CA is mandatory. Importantly, a family member of an individual IA cannot provide distribution services to the same client to whom the IA provides advisory services, ensuring segregation of advisory and distribution activities. Question 2 of 5: Based on the information provided, does Vikram meet the minimum net worth requirement to register as an individual Investment Adviser with SEBI?

answer.text: No, because the minimum net worth required for an individual IA is Rs. 10 lakh
Yes, because the minimum net worth for an individual IA is Rs. 5 lakh, and Vikram has Rs. 8 lakh
No, because the minimum net worth required is Rs. 25 lakh for all categories of IAs
Yes, but only because his wife's income as a distributor can be added to his net worth

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Under SEBI (Investment Advisers) Regulations, 2013, the minimum net worth requirement for an individual Investment Adviser is Rs. 5 lakh. Vikram has a net worth of Rs. 8 lakh, which exceeds this threshold. For non-individual IAs (partnerships, companies, LLPs), the minimum net worth requirement is Rs. 50 lakh — a significantly higher bar.
**The Trap:** Option A inflates the individual IA net worth to Rs. 10 lakh. Option C applies a uniform Rs. 25 lakh requirement which is incorrect — different categories have different thresholds. Option D incorrectly suggests spousal income can be clubbed for net worth calculation, which is not permitted under the regulations.
**Cross-Exam Context:** The distinction between individual IA (Rs. 5 lakh) and non-individual IA (Rs. 50 lakh) net worth requirements is a frequently tested concept. Candidates must remember both thresholds and the triggers for conversion from individual to non-individual status.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When planning to set up an individual IA practice, ensure your personal net worth — excluding your spouse's assets and liabilities — independently meets the Rs. 5 lakh threshold. Keep net worth certificates updated as SEBI may request them during renewal.
